question: In Series 9, Episode 11 of Doctor Who, the Doctor is trapped inside an ever-shifting maze. What is this location called in the official script for the episode? Give the setting exactly as it appears in the first scene heading.

Doctor Who Series 9 Episode 11 official script PDF
Heaven Sent script scene headings maze castle location
working_plan: Find official BBC script or production script for Heaven Sent
Locate the first scene heading describing the maze/castle location
Extract exact wording as it appears in the scene heading
Verify source authenticity to ensure it's the official script

solver.reasoning_summary: Found the official BBC script for Doctor Who Series 9, Episode 11 ('Heaven Sent') written by Steven Moffat. The first scene heading in the script is 'INT. THE CASTLE - DAY1 1'. The location/setting name as it appears in this first scene heading is 'THE CASTLE', which refers to the ever-shifting maze where the Doctor is trapped.

tool_calls.name: sandbox_exec

arguments.code:
# Let me extract and analyze the first few lines of the script to confirm the first scene heading
script_text = """DOCTOR WHO
SERIES 9
EPISODE 11
"Heaven Sent"
by
 STEVEN MOFFAT
  BLUE AMENDMENTS
 24/08/2015
 (SHOOTING BLOCK 6)
(c) BBC Wales 2015
INT. THE CASTLE - DAY1 1
Spires and towers, an ancient sprawling castle."""

lines = script_text.split('\n')
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    print(f"Line {i+1}: '{line.strip()}'")
    if line.strip().startswith('INT.') or line.strip().startswith('EXT.'):
        print(f"  ^^ This is the first scene heading (Line {i+1})")
        break

tool_calls.name: sandbox_exec

arguments.code:
# Let me analyze the first scene heading to extract the setting/location name
scene_heading = "INT. THE CASTLE - DAY1 1"
print(f"Full scene heading: '{scene_heading}'")

# In script format, the structure is typically: INT./EXT. [LOCATION] - [TIME] [additional numbers]
# The location/setting is the part between INT./EXT. and the dash
import re
match = re.match(r'(INT\.|EXT\.)\s+([^-]+)\s*-', scene_heading)
if match:
    location_part = match.group(2).strip()
    print(f"Location/setting part: '{location_part}'")
else:
    print("Could not parse scene heading")

tool_calls.summary:
stdout=Full scene heading: 'INT. THE CASTLE - DAY1 1'
Location/setting part: 'THE CASTLE'

INT. THE CASTLE - DAY1 1
Spires and towers, an ancient sprawling castle.

THE DOCTOR
(V.O.)
As you come into this world, 
something else is also born. 
Pulling back -
We are looking at the sunrise through a row of windows in an 
ancient, stone wall.
THE DOCTOR (cont’d)
(V.O.)
You begin your life and it begins a 
journey - towards you. 
Time lapse photography: as the sun moves in the sky, the 
sunbeams sweep round the room like searchlights...
